sarga.title: Kaikeyi insists on the boons -- Ayodhya is decorated all over--People wait eagerly for the event -- Vasistha arrives at the inner apartment of the king-- Dasaratha sends Sumantra to fetch Rama.

shlokas.original:
पुत्रशोकार्दितं पापा विसंज्ञं पतितं भुवि।
विवेष्टमानमुद्वीक्ष्य सैक्ष्वाकमिदमब्रवीत्।।2.14.1।।
shlokas.translation: The evil woman (Kaikeyi) said to the king of the Ikshvaku race who lay unconscious on the floor writhing and tormented with grief on account of his son.

shlokas.original:
पापं कृत्वैव किमिदं मम संश्रुत्य संश्रवम्। 
शेषे क्षितितले सन्नः स्थित्यां स्थातुं त्वमर्हसि।।2.14.2।।
shlokas.translation: (Kaikeyi said) What is this? After hearing my response to the promised boon, why are you lying down on the floor dejected as if you had committed a sin? It behoves  you to maintain your status.

shlokas.original:
आहु स्सत्यं हि परमं धर्मं धर्मविदो जनाः। 
सत्यमाश्रित्य हि मया त्वं च धर्मं प्रबोधितः।।2.14.3।।
shlokas.translation: Those conversant with righteousness say that truth is a great virtue. By following the truth, I have enlightened you about your duty.

shlokas.original:
संश्रुत्य शैब्यश्श्येनाय स्वां तनुं जगतीपतिः।
प्रदाय पक्षिणे राज न्जगाम गतिमुत्तमाम्।।2.14.4।।
shlokas.translation: O King O lord of the earth (king) Saibya attained the highest state by offering his own body to a hawk in accordance with his promise.

shlokas.original:
तथा ह्यलर्कस्तेजस्वी ब्राह्मणे वेदपारगे। 
याचमाने स्वके नेत्रे उद्धृत्याविमना ददौ।।2.14.5।।
shlokas.translation: In the same way the lustrous king Alarka, on being solicited by a brahmin who was an adept in the Vedas, plucked his own eyes and gave them to him without hesitation.

shlokas.original:
सरितां तु पतिस्स्वल्पां मर्यादां सत्यमन्वितः। 
सत्यानुरोधात्समये स्वां वेलां नातिवर्तते।।2.14.6।।
shlokas.translation: The ocean, lord of rivers, even when (tidal) time comes does not cross the shore in obedience to truth.

shlokas.original:
सत्यमेकपदं ब्रह्म सत्ये धर्मः प्रतिष्टितः।
सत्यमेवाक्षया वेदा सत्येनैवाप्यते स्परम्।।2.14.7।।
shlokas.translation: Truth is Brahman in one word. Righteousness is firmly established in truth. Truth is the imperishable Vedas. The supreme state can be attained by means of truth.

shlokas.original:
सत्यं समनुवर्तस्व यदि धर्मे धृता मतिः।
सफलस्स वरो मेऽस्तु वरदो ह्यसि सत्तम।।2.14.8।।
shlokas.translation: O excellent one, if your mind is firmly fixed in righteousness, then by following it fulfil the boon granted to me.You are the bestower of boons indeed

shlokas.original:
धर्मस्यैहाभिकामार्थं मम चैवाभिचोदनात्। 
प्रव्राजय सुतं रामं त्रिःखलु त्वां ब्रवीम्यहम्।।2.14.9।।
shlokas.translation: You must uphold righteousness. I also urge you to do that. Send your son Rama to the forest. I am repeating this statememt thrice.

shlokas.original:
समयं च ममार्येमं यदि त्वं न करिष्यसि। 
अग्रतस्ते परित्यक्ता परित्यक्ष्यामि जीवितम्।।2.14.10।।
shlokas.translation: O noble one, if you do not fulfil the agreement, it will mean I have been abandoned by you. (Therefore) I will give up my life in your presence.

shlokas.original:
एवं प्रचोदितो राजा कैकेय्या निर्विशङ्कया। 
नाशकत्पाशमुन्मोक्तुं बलिरिन्द्रकृतं यथा।।2.14.11।।
shlokas.translation: In this way urged by Kaikeyi without fear or hesitation, he (Dasaratha), like Bali unable to release himself from the noose cast by Indra (through Vamana), could not get out of the noose of promise.

shlokas.original:
उद्भ्रान्तहृदयश्चापि विवर्णवदनोऽभवत्। 
स धुर्योवैपरिस्पन्दन्युगचक्रान्तरं यथा।।2.14.12।।
shlokas.translation: Like a bullock, restlessly moving between the wheels and the yoke, Dasaratha's  heart started beating wildly and his face turned pale.

shlokas.original:
विह्वलाभ्यां च नेत्राभ्यामपश्यन्निव भूपतिः। 
कृच्छ्राद्धैर्येण संस्तभ्य कैकेयीमिदमब्रवीत्।।2.14.13।।
shlokas.translation: The king was unable to see with his eyes overwhelmed (with tears). Gaining with great difficulty a firm footing and courage he addressed Kaikeyi:

shlokas.original:
यस्ते मन्त्रकृतः पाणिरग्नौ पापे मया धृतः।
तं त्यजामि स्वजं चैव तव पुत्रं त्वया सह।।2.14.14।।
shlokas.translation: O wicked woman, I held your hand amid chantings of mantras with the sacred fire (as witness). I now disown that hand, you and your son begotten by me.

shlokas.original:
प्रयाता रजनी देवि सूर्यस्योदयनंप्रति। 
अभिषेकं गुरुजनस्त्वरयिष���यति मां ध्रुवम्।।2.14.15।।
shlokas.translation: O queen the night has departed and the Sun is about to rise. The preceptors now will definitely ask me to hasten for the consecration.

shlokas.original:
रामाभिषेकसंभारैस्तदर्थमुपकल्पितैः।
रामः कारयितव्यो मे मृतस्य सलिलक्रियाम्।।2.14.16।।
shlokas.translation: With the materials already collected for the consecration of Rama he will perform my funeral rite of offering water to the dead.

shlokas.original:
त्वया सपुत्रया नैव कर्तव्या सलिलक्रिया। 
व्याहन्ताऽस्यशुभाचारे यदि रामाभिषेचनम्।।2.14.17।।
shlokas.translation: O woman of inauspicious practices, since you are obstructing Rama's coronation, you and your son shall not perform my funeral rite of offering water to the dead.

shlokas.original:
न च शक्तोऽस्म्यहं द्रष्टुं दृष्ट्वा पूर्वं तथासुखम्। 
हतहर्षं निरानन्दं पुनर्जनमवाङ्मुखम्।।2.14.18।।
shlokas.translation: Having seen people formerly enjoying happiness, I cannot see them again with their downcast, cheerless faces devoid of all happiness.

shlokas.original:
तां तथा ब्रुवतस्तस्य भूमिपस्य महात्मनः। 
प्रभाता शर्वरी पुण्या चन्द्रनक्षत्रशालिनी।।2.14.19।।
shlokas.translation: While the king was thus speaking to her (Kaikeyi) that holy night resplendent with the Moon and the stars, the day dawned.

shlokas.original:
ततः पापसमाचारा कैकेयी पार्थिवं पुनः। 
उवाच परुषं वाक्यं वाक्यज्ञा रोषमूर्छिता।।2.14.20।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, Kaikeyi of sinful conduct and skilful in the use of words, rendered senseless by wrath, again addressing the king in harsh words said:

shlokas.original:
किमिदं भाषसे राजन्वाक्यं गररुजोपमम्। 
आनाययितुमक्लिष्टं पुत्रं राममिहार्हसि।।2.14.21।।
shlokas.translation: O king, why are you speaking these painful, poisonous words? What is this? It  behoves you to summon your son Rama who does not expearience this pain.

shlokas.original:
स्थाप्य राज्ये मम सुतं कृत्वा रामं वनेचरम्। 
निस्सपत्नां च मां कृत्वा कृतकृत्यो भविष्यसि।।2.14.22।।
shlokas.translation: Only after you have  installed my son in the kingdom and sent Rama to the forest and  made me free from rivals, you shall have your duty fulfiled.

shlokas.original:
स नुन्न इव तीक्ष्णेन प्रतोदेन हयोत्तमः।
राजा प्रचोदितोऽभीक्ष्णं कैकेयीमिदमब्रवीत्।।2.14.23।।
shlokas.translation: The king who was very much incited by her words, like a horse of the best breed whipped by the lash, replied to Kaikeyi:

shlokas.original:
धर्मबन्धेन बध्दोऽस्मि नष्टा च मम चेतना। 
ज्येष्ठं पुत्रं प्रियं रामं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि धार्मिकम्।।2.14.24।।
shlokas.translation: I am bound by bond of righteousness. My intellect is destroyed. I wish to see my beloved, virtuous firstborn, Rama.

shlokas.original:
ततः प्रभातां रजनीमुदिते च दिवाकरे।
पुण्ये नक्षत्रयोगे च मुहूर्ते च समाहिते।।2.14.25।।
वसिष्ठो गुणसम्पन्न श्शिष्यै परिवृतस्तदा। 
उपगृह्याशु सम्भारान्प्रविवेश पुरोत्तमम्।।2.14.26।।
shlokas.translation: The night dawned. Ths Sun rose. The auspicious time with the sacred star in (favourable) conjunction arrived. The virtuous Vasistha, surrounded by his disciples, soon entered the best of abodes with the articles collected (for the ceremony).

shlokas.original:
सिक्तसम्मार्जितपथां पताकोत्तम भूषिताम्।
विचित्रकुसुमाकीर्णां नानास्रग्भिर्विराजिताम्।।2.14.27।।
संहृष्टमनुजोपेतां समृद्धविपणापणाम्। 
महोत्सवसमाकीर्णां राघवार्थे समुत्सुकाम्।।2.14.28।।
चन्दनागरुधूपैश्च सर्वतः प्रतिधूपिताम्। 
तां पुरीं समतिक्रम्य पुरन्दरपुरोपमाम्।।।2.14.29।।
ददर्शान्तःपुरश्रेष्ठं नानाद्विजगणायुतम्।
पौरजानपदाकीर्णं ब्राह्मणैरुपशोभितम्।।2.14.30।।	
यज्ञविद्भि स्सुसम्पूर्णं सदस्यैः परमद्विजैः।
shlokas.translation: The streets in Ayodhya were swept and sprinkled with water, decorated with excellent flags, strewn with flowers of variegated colours and bedecked with various flower garlands. The streets were crowded with curious people rejoicing at the event. The stalls and markets were abundantly filled with merchandise.The city was full of great festivities. The people were curious to see Rama. Sandalwood and incense were burnt and fragrance pervaded in all the directions. The city resembled Amaravati, the abode of Indra. Vasistha entered the city, covered some distance and beheld the best inner apartment. There he saw host of brahmins, citizens and villagers. The best of inner apartments was graced with brahmins who were experts in sacrificial rituals priests and eminent  brahmins.

shlokas.original:
तदन्तःपुरमासाद्य व्यतिचक्राम तं जनम्।।2.14.31।।
वसिष्ठः परमप्रीतः परमर्षिर्विवेश च।
shlokas.translation: Having reached the inner apartment, the eminent ascetic, Vasistha felt very happy. He passed by the people and entered the inner apartment.

shlokas.original:
सत्वपश्यद्विनिष्क्रान्तं सुमन्त्रं नाम सारथिम्।।2.14.32।।
द्वारे मनुजसिंहस्य सचिवं प्रियदर्शनम्।
shlokas.translation: Sage Vasistha saw handsome Sumantra, minister to Dasaratha, a lion among the charioteers coming out of the gate.

shlokas.original:
तमुवाच महातेजा स्सूतपुत्रं विशारदम्।।2.14.33 
वसिष्ठः क्षिप्रमाचक्ष्व नृपतेर्मामिहागतम्।
shlokas.translation: Effulgent Vasistha said to the expert chariotieer Sumantra to inform the king immediately of his arrival.

shlokas.original:
इमे गङ्गोदकघटा स्सागरेभ्यश्च काञ्चनाः।।2.14.34।।
औदुम्बरं भद्रपीठमभिषेकार्थमागतम्।
सर्वबीजानि गन्धाश्च रत्नानि विविधानि च।।2.14.35।।
क्षौद्रं दधि घृतं लाजा दर्भास्सुमनसः पयः। 
अष्टौ च कन्या रुचिरा मत्तश्च वरवारणः।।2.14.36।।
चतुरश्वो रथश्श्रीमान्निस्त्रिंशो धनुरुत्तमम्। 
वाहनं नरसंयुक्तं छत्रं च शशिसन्निभम्।।2.14.37।।
श्वेते च वालव्यजने भृङ्गारुश्च हिरण्मयः। 
हेमदामपिनध्दश्च ककुद्मान्पाण्डुरो वृषः।।2.14.38।।
केसरी च चतुर्दंष्ट्रो हरिश्रेष्ठो महाबलः। 
सिंहासनं व्याघ्रतनु स्समिद्धश्च हुताशनः।।2.14.39।।
सर्ववादित्रसङ्घाश्च वेश्याश्चालङ्कृता स्स्त्रयः। 
आचार्या ब्राह्मणा गावः पुण्याश्च मृगपक्षिणः।।2.14.40।।
पौरजानपदश्रेष्ठा नैगमाश्च गणै स्सह। 
एते चान्ये च बहवो नीयमानाः प्रियंवदाः।।2.14.41।।
अभिषेकाय रामस्य सह तिष्ठन्ति पार्थिवैः।
shlokas.translation: Here are the pots filled with water from river Ganga and golden vessels with water from the seas. And throne made of udumbara wood for the installation ceremony. All kinds of seeds, perfumes, different kinds of precious stones, honey, curd, ghee, puffed grains, kusha grass (special grass used in rituals), flowers, milk, eight beautiful girls, an intoxicated elephant, a dignified chariot drawn by four horses, a sword, an elegant bow, a palanquin accompanied by bearers, an umbrella resembling the full Moon, two white fans made of Yak's tails, a golden vessel, a palecoloured humped bull, wearing golden gerland, a lion having four strong and large teeth, a mighty horse of the best breed, a throne, tiger skin, fire kindled with faggots, all kinds of musical instruments, courtesans, welldecorated women, preceptors, brahmins, cows, sacred animals and birds, eminent citizens and villagers, groups of merchants all speaking pleasant words and kings -- all are here for Rama's coronation.

shlokas.original:
त्वरयस्व महाराजं यथा समुदितेऽहनि।।2.14.42।।
पुष्ये नक्षत्रयोगे च रामो राज्यमवाप्नुयात्।
shlokas.translation: Hasten the maharaja as Rama has to be installed in the kingdom as soon as the day breaks under the conjunction of the sacred Pushya star.

shlokas.original:
इति तस्य वच श्श्रुत्वा सूतपुत्रो महात्मनः।।2.14.43।।
स्तुवन्नृपतिशार्दूलं प्रविवेश निवेशनम्।
shlokas.translation: Having heard the words of the great Vasistha, charioteer Sumantra entered the   inner apartment, singing the praise of this tiger among kings (Dasaratha).

shlokas.original:
तं तु पूर्वोदितं वृध्दं द्वारस्था राज सम्मतम्।।2.14.44।।
न शेकुरभिसंरोध्दुं राज्ञः प्रियचिकीर्षवः।
shlokas.translation: The doorkeepers, beloved wellwishers of the king, did not detain aged Sumantra  who was liked by the king and who had arrived early.

shlokas.original:
स समीपस्थितो राज्ञस्तामवस्थामजज्ञिवान्।।2.14.45।।
वाग्भिः परमतुष्टाभिरभिष्टोतुं प्रचक्रमे।
shlokas.translation: Sumantra approached the king unaware of the state of his mind and commenced praising him in highly pleasing words (as usual).

shlokas.original:
तत स्सूतो यथाकालं पार्थिवस्य निवेशने।।2.14.46।।
सुमन्त्रः प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा तुष्टाव जगतीपतिम्।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter the charioteer Sumantra entered the inner apartment of the king, and with folded hands began singing the praise of the lord of the earth appropriate to the hour.

shlokas.original:
यथा नन्दति तेजस्वी सागरो भास्करोदये।।2.14.47।।
प्रीतः प्रीतेन मनसा तथाऽनन्दघन स्स्वतः।
shlokas.translation: Just as the mighty ocean rejoices at sunrise, the inherently cheerful Sumantra became delighted to see the king.

shlokas.original:
इन्द्रमस्यां तु वेलायामभितुष्टाव मातलिः।।2.14.48।।
सोऽजयद्दानवान्सर्वांस्तथा त्वां बोधयाम्यहम्।
shlokas.translation: Just as Indra's charioteer at this time (at dawn) extols him, the conquerer of demons, so do I rouse you (with a song).

shlokas.original:
वेदास्सहाङ्गविद्याश्च यथाह्यात्मभुवं विभुम्।।2.14.49। 
ब्रह्माणं बोधयन्त्यद्य तथा त्वां बोधयाम्यहम्।
shlokas.translation: Just as the Vedas and Vedangas awaken the selfborn lord Brahma, so do I  awaken you.

shlokas.original:
आदित्यस्सह चन्द्रेण यथा भूतधरां शुभाम्।।2.14.50।।
बोधयत्यद्य पृथिवीं तथा त्वां बोधयाम्यहम्।
shlokas.translation: As the Sun along with the Moon awakens the auspicious earth which supports all  beings, in the same way I am awakening you today.

shlokas.original:
उत्तिष्ठाशु महाराज कृतकौतुकमङ्गलः।।2.14.51।।
विराजमानो वपुषा मेरोरिव दिवाकरः।
shlokas.translation: O mighty king get up at once like the Sun rising up mount Meru. Put on your beautiful body auspicious robes befitting the festivity.

shlokas.original:
सोमसूर्यौ च काकुत्स्थ शिववैश्रवणावपि।।2.14.52।।
वरुणश्चाग्निरिन्द्रश्च विजयं प्रदिशन्तु ते।
shlokas.translation: O descendent of Kakutstha, may Moon and Sun, Siva and Kubera, Varuna, Agni and Indra bestow victory on you.

shlokas.original:
गता भगवती रात्रिः कृतं कृत्यमिदं तव।।2.14.53।।
बुद्ध्यस्व नृपशार्दूल कुरु कार्यमनन्तरम्। 
उदतिष्ठत रामस्य समग्रमभिषेचनम्।।2.14.54।।
shlokas.translation: O best among kings the holy night has passed. Having been acquainted with the acts accomplished so far, perform whatever is further necessary for the installation    ceremony. All preparations for Rama's coronation ceremony have since been complete.

shlokas.original:
पौरजानापदैश्चापि नैगमैश्च कृताञ्जलिः। 
स्वयं वशिष्ठो भगवान्ब्राह्मणै स्सह तिष्ठति।।2.14.55।।
shlokas.translation: Venerable Vasistha along with inhabitants of the city and countryside including merchants and brahmins with folded hands awaits you.

shlokas.original:
क्षिप्रमाज्ञाप्यतां राजन्राघवस्याभिषेचनम्। 
यथा ह्यपालाः पशवो यथा सेना ह्यनायका।।2.14.56।।
यथा चन्द्रं विना रात्रिर्यथा गावो विना वृषम्। 
एवं हि भविता राष्ट्रं यत्र राजा न दृश्यते।।2.14.57।।
shlokas.translation: O king Rama's installation may be promptly ordered. As herds without herdsman, army without commander, night without the Moon, and cows without a bull, so is a kingdom without a king.

shlokas.original:
इति तस्य वच श्शृत्वा सान्त्वपूर्वमिवार्थवत्। 
अभ्यकीर्यत शोकेन भूय एव महीपतिः।।2.14.58।।
shlokas.translation: Hearing these  meaningful words of Sumantra uttered in an appealing tone, the lord of the earth relapsed into grief.

shlokas.original:
तत स्सराजा तं सूतं सन्नहर्ष स्सुतं प्रति। 
शोकरक्तेक्षण श्श्रीमानुद्वीक्ष्योवाच धार्मिकः।।2.14.59।।
वाक्यैस्तु खलु मर्माणि मम भूयो निकृन्तसि।
shlokas.translation: The virtuous and glorious king thinking of his son without cheer looked upward with eyes red with tears and said to the charioteer, You are causing my heart excruciating pain with your words.

shlokas.original:
सुमन्त्रः करुणं श्रुत्वा दृष्ट्वा दीनं च पार्थिवम्। 
प्रगृहीताञ्जलिः किञ्चित्तस्माद्देशादपाक्रमत्।।2.14.60।।
shlokas.translation: Having heard the sorrowful words of the king and having seen his wretched state  Sumantra with palms folded moved away a little from the place.

shlokas.original:
यदा वक्तुं स्वयं दैन्यान्न शशाक महीपतिः।।2.14.61।।
तदा सुमन्त्रं मन्त्रज्ञा कैकेयी प्रत्युवाच ह।
shlokas.translation: When the king himself in that wretched state was not able to speak to Sumantra, crafty Kaikeyi said to him in reply:

shlokas.original:
सुमन्त्र राजा रजनीं रामहर्षसमुत्सुकः।।2.14.62।।
���्रजागरपरिश्रान्तो निद्राया वशमुपेयिवान्।
shlokas.translation: O Sumantra, excited with joy on account of the (proposed) installation of Rama, the king did not have a wink of sleep all night. He is weary and overpowered with sleep.

shlokas.original:
तद्गच्छ त्वरितं सूत राजपुत्रं यशस्विनम्।।2.14.63।।
राममानय भद्रं ते नात्र कार्या विचारणा।
shlokas.translation: So, O Sumantra quickly fetch the gentle, illustrious prince Rama. Let there be no hesitation in this matter.

shlokas.original:
स मन्यमानः कल्याणं हृदयेन ननन्द च।।2.14.64।।
निर्जगाम च सम्प्रीत्या त्वरितो राजशासनात्।
shlokas.translation: Thinking that an auspicious event will take place, he rejoiced at heart and in obedience to royal order, left at once.

shlokas.original:
सुमन्त्रश्चिन्तयामास त्वरितं चोदितस्तया।।2.14.65।।
व्यक्तं रामोऽभिषेकार्थमिहायास्यति धर्मवित्।
shlokas.translation: Urged by Kaikeyi, Sumantra thought 'Righteous Rama will definitely come here for the purpose of  installation'.

shlokas.original:
इति सूतो मतिं कृत्वा हर्षेण महताऽऽवृतः।।2.14.66।।
निर्जगाम महाबाहो राघवस्य दिदृक्षया।
shlokas.translation: So thought the charioteer and filled with great delight left (the inner apartment), eager to see the mightyarmed Rama.

shlokas.original:
सागरह्रदसङ्काशात्सुमन्त्रोऽन्तःपुराच्छुभात्।।2.14.67।।
निष्क्रम्य जनसम्बाधं ददर्श द्वारमग्रतः।
shlokas.translation: Sumantra, departed from the auspcious inner apartment which looked like the deep waters of the sea and saw people thronging the gate.

shlokas.original:
ततः पुरस्तात्सहसा विनिर्गतो महीपतीन्द्वारगतो विलोकयन्। 
ददर्श पौरान्विविधान्महाधना नुपस्थितान्द्वारमुपेत्य विष्ठितान्।।2.14.68।।
shlokas.translation: Then Sumantra hastened towards the gate where he saw kings, wealthy people and citizens who had already arrived and waiting there.    इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अयोध्याकाण्डे चतुर्दशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fourteenth sarga of Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
